preamble: L-vocalization is a phonetic process in which an alveolar lateral approximant [l] is replaced by a labio-velar approximant [w] or a similar rounded vowel/glide.

examples.note: Vocalization of dark-L in syllable-final position.
Shift of the historical velarized lateral to a labio-velar approximant.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
